articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Beating the Market in Zimbabwe: Why Marketing and Sales Capabilities Are the New Competitive Edge
more.description: IN Zimbabwe’s volatile and often unforgiving business environment, consistent growth is difficult. Sustained outperformance is even rarer. more.title: Real-time digital traffic surveillance systems
more.description: GOVERNMENT has concluded the development of an automated smart traffic management system capable of detecting road violations in real time, and is ready to launch it on a full scale across the country. more.title: Zimbabwe Projects Cereal Surplus as Soyabean Output Surges
more.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe is projecting a significant cereal surplus of up to 964,945 metric tonnes by March 2027, following a strong recovery in agricultural output during the 2025/2026 summer season, according to a Cabinet report presented this week. The update, delivered by the Ministry of Agriculture to Cabinet on 21 April, estimates total cereal production […]The post Zimbabwe Projects Cereal Surplus as Soyabean Output Surges appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.description: Zimbabwe’s deepening informalisation is no longer a peripheral labour-market adjustment; it is a structural macroeconomic condition shaped by currency instability, fiscal design, institutional credibility, and the reconfiguration of global capital flows. more.title: From Data Scarcity to Data Sovereignty: How Zimbabwean Business Can Leapfrog into the AI Age
more.description: IN boardrooms across Harare, Bulawayo, and Victoria Falls, a quiet but consequential shift is underway. more.title: Green Ambitions, Hard Realities: Reimagining Innovation and Sustainable Business Building in Zimbabwe
more.description: Zimbabwe stands at an unusual intersection of necessity and opportunity. On one hand, the pressures are unmistakable—energy insecurity, climate variability, industrial stagnation, and constrained capital. On the other, the country possesses the raw ingredients of a green transition: abundant sunlight, agricultural depth, mineral wealth, and a youthful population increasingly attuned to technology and sustainability. The […]The post Green Ambitions, Hard Realities: Reimagining Innovation and Sustainable Business Building in Zimbabwe appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Land Incentives Framework Sparks Political Storm as Critics Accuse Kudakwashe Tagwirei of Overreach
more.description: HARARE — Zimbabwe’s newly announced land purchase incentives framework—positioned by the government as a tool to broaden access to land and support agricultural productivity—has triggered a wave of backlash from opposition figures, former ruling party insiders, and governance watchdogs, who allege abuse of office, lack of transparency, and creeping state capture.
